page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 31 GB of system memory, providing 7.75 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage.

Benchmark data reveals highly polarized performance characteristics. The server demonstrates top-tier single-threaded capabilities, with gzip compression and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 10%, and stress-ng single-core performance placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core scaling is a notable tradeoff; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is also split, showing average cached read bandwidth of 334.26 GB/sec but weak uncached large-block read and write speeds.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high memory density per core without massive multi-threaded compute demands. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage makes it suitable for memory-intensive applications, single-threaded text processing, Redis caching operations, and lightweight database tasks that benefit from fast local scratch space but do not require extensive parallel processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 4 vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon 6985P-C cores, 31 GB of RAM, and a 137 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating at 2.3 GHz with a 504 MB L3 cache, it supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with single-threaded gzip compression and text processing in the top 10%, though multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 25%. This memory-optimized profile, combined with dedicated CPU allocation and local SSD storage, provides qualitative resource density for memory-bound, single-threaded tasks, Redis caching, and lightweight database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and a 137 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd features 4 vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and a 137 GB local NVMe SSD for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, local NVMe SSD storage, and strong single-threaded performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores) and 504 MB L3 cache
31 GB of system memory providing a high 7.75 GB RAM-to-core ratio
Bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD for low-latency storage operations
Top-tier single-threaded performance in gzip compression and text processing
Tradeoffs include weak multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth
Suited for memory-bound applications, Redis caching, and lightweight databases
